Prompt
U
Oracle Events
1. Search Airtable for Noah Fitzgerald's relocation record using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table: tblRelocations01, query: "Noah Fitzgerald"). Find recReloc00000006: Noah Fitzgerald, GreenStack Energy, Status: Completed, Origin: Philadelphia PA, Destination: Seattle WA, Move Start: 2026-04-28, Move End: 2026-04-29, Coordinator: Fatimah Al-Rashidi, Special Requirements: empty.
2. Search Airtable for Noah Fitzpatrick using airtable_search_records (table: tblRelocations01, query: "Noah Fitzpatrick" or "Axiom"). No record exists - Fitzpatrick's relocation was arranged via email/Slack without formal Airtable tracking.
3. Search emails for Noah Fitzgerald booking confirmations using search_emails (query: "Noah Fitzgerald" or "Fitzgerald Seattle"). Find: (a) Fitzgerald's preference email requesting Capitol Hill/First Hill area, $1,800-$2,400/month budget, fly out April 27 before starting April 29; (b) Fatimah's apartment request to Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest - confirmed Option A: 412 Boylston Ave E, Apt 3B, Capitol Hill ($2,100/month); (c) Fatimah's flight booking request to Derek Saunders at Atlas - booked for April 14 instead of the requested April 27. The flight date is wrong.
4. Search emails for Noah Fitzpatrick booking confirmations using search_emails (query: "Noah Fitzpatrick" or "Fitzpatrick" or "Axiom"). Find: (a) Carla Mendenhall (Axiom HR) introducing Fitzpatrick - senior engineer, PHL→SEA, fully flexible April 2026; (b) Fitzpatrick's preferences - any April date, furnished 1BR; (c) Fatimah's confirmation to Fitzpatrick: apartment at 412 Boylston Ave E, Apt 3B, Capitol Hill - same apartment as Fitzgerald. Flight: ACT-77492, Alaska Airlines AS 422, April 14. Both Noahs were given the same apartment and the same flight confirmation.
5. Search emails for the flight confirmation details using search_emails (query: "ACT-77492" or "Alaska Airlines AS 422"). Find one booking: Confirmation ACT-77492, Passenger: Noah Fitzgerald, Alaska Airlines AS 422, April 14, PHL→SEA. This single booking was referenced in both Noahs' packages - Fitzgerald's flight is on April 14 (Fitzpatrick's date) instead of his requested April 27.
6. Search Slack for Noah discussion threads using conversations_search_messages (query: "Fitzpatrick" or "Noah" or "Axiom"). Find thread in #operations (C006): Emeka flagged both Noahs going to Seattle in April, Fatimah asked "Fitzpatrick or Fitzgerald?", Emeka confirmed "Fitzpatrick = Axiom, Fitzgerald = GreenStack." Also find Fatimah's update: "Axiom/Fitzpatrick intake complete."
7. Search CRM for GreenStack deal using crm_search_deals (query: "GreenStack"). Find deal_greenstack_q2: $13,500, mentions Priya Venkatesh and Noah Fitzgerald relocations.
8. Search CRM for Axiom using crm_search_companies (query: "Axiom") or crm_search_deals (query: "Axiom"). Find company_axiom (Axiom Precision Manufacturing, Detroit, 900 employees, type: Prospect) but no Axiom deal in CRM.
9. Search Linear for Noah Fitzgerald tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "Fitzgerald" or "GreenStack"). Find 3 tickets: #316 Apartment Search (budget $1,800-$2,400), #317 Flight Booking (target April 27, confirming booked date of April 14 is wrong), #318 Moving Coordination (vendor Swift Relocations, target April 28-29).
10. Search Linear for Noah Fitzpatrick tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "Fitzpatrick" or "Axiom"). No tickets exist despite Slack references to a filed intake ticket.
11. Look up Emeka Diallo's email using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Emeka"). Find emeka.diallo@moveops.com.
12. Look up Chloe Vance's email using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Chloe"). Find chloe.vance@moveops.com.
13. Create Airtable record for Noah Fitzpatrick using airtable_create_record (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table: tblRelocations01, fields: Name: Noah Fitzpatrick, Company: Axiom Precision Manufacturing, Status: In Progress, Origin: Philadelphia PA, Destination: Seattle WA, Coordinator: Fatimah Al-Rashidi).
14. Update Noah Fitzgerald's Airtable record to flag the mix-up using airtable_update_records (recReloc00000006, Special Requirements noting: wrong flight date booked April 14 instead of April 27, apartment 412 Boylston Ave E Apt 3B double-assigned to both Noahs).
15. Create a Linear tracking ticket for the mix-up resolution using linear_create_issue (title: "URGENT - Noah Fitzgerald/Fitzpatrick Mix-Up", description covering: flight ACT-77492 booked April 14 instead of April 27, same apartment assigned to both, Fitzpatrick missing from Airtable/Linear/CRM, priority: urgent).
16. Add comments to Fitzgerald's existing Linear tickets (#316 and/or #317) flagging the apartment and flight mix-ups using linear_create_comment.
17. Send email to Emeka Diallo using send_email (to: emeka.diallo@moveops.com) explaining: wrong flight date (April 14 vs April 27), double-assigned apartment (412 Boylston Ave E Apt 3B), missing Fitzpatrick tracking records, and vendor course-correction needed.
18. Post in #operations using conversations_add_message (channel: C006) alerting Chloe and the team - two Seattle relocations crossed, vendors may have acted on incorrect information, course-correction needed with Atlas Corporate Travel (flight) and UrbanNest (apartment).

2 Noah Fitzgerald's Airtable record (recReloc00000006) was updated (via airtable_update_records) to flag the identified mix-ups (wrong flight date, double-assigned apartment, or similar).
outcome

✓Run #5The agent called airtable_update_records referencing record id 'recReloc00000006' (Noah Fitzgerald/GreenStack), changing Status from 'Completed' to 'In Progress' and adding detailed audit flags about the flight date error (April 14 vs April 27), double-assigned apartment (412 Boylston), and missing moving coordination.
✗Run #6The agent updated Linear tickets #316 and #317 (via linear_update_issue) to flag mix-ups, but there is no airtable_update_records call found in the trajectory updating Fitzgerald's Airtable record (recReloc00000006) to reflect the booking errors.
3 A Linear issue was created (via linear_create_issue) as an urgent tracking ticket for the Noah Fitzgerald/Fitzpatrick mix-up resolution.
outcome

✓Run #3The agent called linear_create_issue with title referencing both Noahs and priority=1 (urgent). The ticket was successfully created as issue #397.
✓Run #4Call 53 (linear_create_issue) created an urgent (Priority 1) issue titled 'URGENT: Cross-Booking Mix-Up — Fitzgerald (GreenStack) / Fitzpatrick (Axiom) Seattle Relocations' with a detailed description of all 4 mix-ups.
✓Run #5The agent called linear_create_issue and the result confirms issue #397 was created with title 'URGENT: Fitzgerald/Fitzpatrick Seattle relocation mix-up — corrections needed' with full description and corrective checklist.
✓Run #6The agent called linear_create_issue with a title referencing both 'Fitzgerald' and 'Fitzpatrick' as an urgent tracking ticket describing the mix-ups.
4 An message was sent (via send_email or conversations_add_message, or similar messaging tool) to Emeka Diallo explaining the mix-ups between the two Noah relocations.
outcome
